about.title: Helping You Achieve Better Health Naturally
about.description:
Dr. Maegan has been practicing Chinese Medicine in Asheville for ten years. Her early path began with her love of nature, nutrition, and herbal medicine as ways to find peace and healing in her life. She received her yoga teaching certificate in 2008 in Philadelphia, which expanded her ideas of health and introduced alternative views of wellness that greatly informs how she lives and practices medicine today. 

After graduating from Green Mountain College in 2012 with a degree in Environmental Studies (education-focused), she joined Americorps for two years. She taught and developed Farm-ToSchool programs in Vermont, focusing on nutrition education, school gardening, organic farming, environmental science, and creating thriving local food systems. 

She then attended a five-year Masters and Doctoral program at Daoist Traditions College of Chinese Medical Arts. Studies focused on the Classical teachings of Chinese medicine with 88th generation Daoist Priest, Jeffrey Yuen. She also undertook advanced studies in orthopedics, women's health and obstetrics, and cardiology in the doctoral program. 

Dr. Maegan is a primary care acupuncturist with the ability to treat a diverse set of conditions. Her specialties include orthopedic (“dry needling”) for acute and chronic pain, psychological conditions, and hormonal imbalances. She is skilled in treating the “mysterious” illnesses that biomedicine struggles to successfully resolve, such as Long Covid,chronic fatigue, autoimmune conditions, and fibromyalgia. Dr. Maegan also has longterm, mentored experienced in supporting those going through cancer treatment. Additionally,she has studied death medicine and is well versed in end of life care. 

As a Chinese medicine doctor, she is committed to seeing her patient as an individual and not a disease, offering compassionate and effective care to every person. It is her greatest honor in life to hold space for all of those that step into her treatment room. She is dedicated to meeting each patient with the utmost sensitivity, care, and respect. 

Dr. Maegan’s other interests include Cha Dao/tea ceremony, gardening, travel, and hiking in the beautiful Appalachian mountains with her dogs.

reviews.name: Kim M.
reviews.quote: Dr. Maegan Davis is amazing. I instantly felt welcomed and heard. She has a calm presence and I always feel better after a treatment with her.

reviews.name: Savannah S.
reviews.quote: I have been getting acupuncture with Maegan and it has been life-changing. My body and mind feel more balanced and at ease. My sleep, anxiety, depression, digestion, stress levels, and energy levels are worlds better. I recommend her to everyone I know. She has a super calm and loving energy and is very easy to connect with. She is a great listener, and truly cares about you and what you have to share.

reviews.name: Anita F.
reviews.quote: Maegan is so very caring and competent, always explaining what's going on so that I understand it. Thank you so much! Anita

contact.title: Ready To Feel Better Naturally?
contact.subtitle: Whether you're seeking pain relief, stress reduction, or better overall wellness, we're here to help. Schedule your consultation today.

faq.title: Questions We Hear Most Often
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before your first visit.

questions.question: Does acupuncture hurt?
questions.answer: Most patients experience little to no discomfort. The needles are extremely thin and many people find treatments relaxing.

questions.question: What conditions can acupuncture treat?
questions.answer: Acupuncture can help with pain, stress, anxiety, headaches, digestive issues, women's health, insomnia, allergies, and many chronic conditions.

questions.question: How long is a treatment?
questions.answer: Most appointments last between 60 and 90 minutes depending on your treatment plan.

questions.question: How many treatments will I need?
questions.answer: This varies by condition. Acute issues may improve in a few visits while chronic conditions generally require ongoing care.
